summary: A GUI application for creating waypoint-driven drone and small-aircraft scenarios, running simulations, replaying trajectories over terrain, analyzing run outputs, and tuning vehicle/controller parameters.

recommended_stack.initial_gui: Panel web app with Plotly, Bokeh, Tabulator, and background worker queues
recommended_stack.initial_simulation_backend: Existing in-house 6DOF quadcopter MPC package under simulations/6DOF_Quadcopter_MPC-main/drone_mc
recommended_stack.map_and_terrain: Extract reusable ESRI World Imagery and AWS Terrarium elevation tile logic from terrain_3d.py
recommended_stack.future_geospatial_visualization: Cesium or MapLibre via Python widget integration once scenario and terrain contracts are stable
recommended_stack.future_quad_fidelity: In-house rotor/actuator/wind extensions first, optional PyBullet/PyFlyt backend second
recommended_stack.future_fixed_wing: JSBSim Python backend using a Cessna 172-class aircraft model

architecture.style: Modular services with typed data contracts and pluggable simulation backends
flow: GUI edits map, waypoints, markers, vehicle parameters, and run settings.
Scenario Manager validates and persists scenario inputs.
Simulation Manager resolves the requested backend through DroneFactory.
Backend returns a normalized RunResult independent of simulator internals.
Run Store persists metadata and time-series outputs.
Replay and Analysis Views load saved runs without rerunning the simulator.

local.purpose: Original product concept, factories, map model, GUI create/render modes, waypoint and marker requirements.
Reusable tile math, ESRI imagery fetch, AWS Terrarium elevation decode, local meter conversion, Plotly terrain rendering.
Pure run_simulation(cfg) -> SimResult entry point for the initial quadcopter backend.
Current SimConfig and MCConfig dataclasses with MPC, vehicle, and Monte Carlo knobs.
Existing Panel app pattern for background execution, progress events, and live plot refresh.
Existing waypoint source modes, editable table, Bokeh point drawing, and sidebar parameter layout.
ProcessPoolExecutor runner with callbacks, progress updates, result streaming, and cancellation.
MATLAB/Simulink reference for future path-following/control conversion.
MATLAB/Simulink reference for future direct path trajectory generation and animation behavior.

external.name: JSBSim Python documentation
external.purpose: Open-source 6DOF flight dynamics model with Python bindings for fixed-wing aircraft.

external.name: JSBSim GitHub
external.purpose: Source, aircraft data, scripts, Cessna examples, and property-tree based integration model.

external.name: gym-pybullet-drones
external.purpose: PyBullet-based quadrotor simulator with steppable physics, multi-agent support, and aerodynamic effects.

external.name: PyFlyt
external.purpose: Bullet/Gymnasium UAV simulation platform with modular quadrotor and fixed-wing concepts.

external.name: Panel Plotly pane
external.purpose: Plotly click, hover, selection, viewport, and efficient update support in Panel apps.

external.name: mapwidget
external.purpose: Future Cesium, MapLibre, Leaflet, OpenLayers, and DeckGL map widgets for Python dashboards.

external.name: Cesium drones use case
external.purpose: Reference for 3D geospatial drone flight visualization, flight logs, terrain, and digital-twin workflows.

data_contracts.MapSpec: Map center, radius, resolution, tile sources, cache key, and vertical exaggeration.
data_contracts.WaypointSet: WGS84 lat/lon/alt waypoints plus local x/y/z coordinates, timing/speed metadata, and smoothing settings.
data_contracts.MarkerSet: Map annotations with geographic/local coordinates, labels, style, visibility, and optional analysis roles.
data_contracts.DroneModelSpec: Vehicle identity, type, mass/inertia/aero/rotor parameters, controller settings, and constraints.
data_contracts.ScenarioSpec: Versioned composition of map, vehicle, waypoints, markers, environment, and default run configuration.
data_contracts.RunConfig: Backend selection, time-step/horizon/worker settings, Monte Carlo perturbations, and deterministic seed.
data_contracts.RunResult: Normalized time-series state/control/reference/error outputs, summary stats, metadata, units, and backend info.

phases.name: Repository and artifact baseline
objectives: Create a project-level package layout around existing prototypes without breaking them.
Define artifact directories for maps, scenarios, runs, exports, and docs.
Produce docs/drone_sim_implementation_plan.json.
implementation_notes: Keep simulations/6DOF_Quadcopter_MPC-main intact as a reference/vendor source until the adapter is stable.
Use dataclass contracts first to avoid adding dependencies before the package shape settles.
risks: Prematurely moving reference code could break the existing GUI.
success_criteria: JSON artifact exists and top-level package can be imported.

phases.name: Application skeleton and state model
objectives: Implement typed specs for maps, waypoints, markers, vehicle models, run configs, run results, and scenarios.
Implement app state transitions for creating scenarios, editing waypoints/markers, running simulations, replaying runs, and comparing runs.
implementation_notes: Move state into service classes rather than coupling business logic to Panel callbacks.
Use local meters internally while preserving WGS84 coordinates in scenario files.
risks: Coordinate frame ambiguity between lat/lon/alt, local x/y/z, and simulator axes.
success_criteria: Scenarios can be created, validated, saved, and loaded from JSON.

phases.name: Map and terrain service
objectives: Extract tile math, bounding boxes, zoom selection, tile downloading, terrain decoding, crop/resample, and coordinate conversion from terrain_3d.py.
Add tile and processed terrain caching.
Support map loading/fetching by lat, lon, radius, and resolution.
implementation_notes: Start with Plotly/Bokeh-compatible arrays and figures because they match the current dependency set.
Add Cesium/MapLibre only after terrain and scenario persistence are stable.
risks: Tile providers may rate-limit or change access patterns.
Large terrain resolutions can create heavy HTML and memory usage.
success_criteria: A MapAsset can be created from a MapSpec and used for coordinate conversion.

phases.name: ScenarioFactory and creation GUI
objectives: Implement ScenarioManager/ScenarioFactory operations for create, validate, save, load, duplicate, and export.
Expose map picker/fetcher, waypoint editing, marker editing, metadata, validation, and save/load controls in the GUI.
Extend waypoints from x/y to lat/lon/alt with conversion to local x/y/z before simulation.
implementation_notes: Treat scenarios as reusable inputs and runs as output artifacts attached to scenarios.
Keep sidebar modes for trajectory and marker editing as specified in idea.txt.
risks: A large callback graph can become hard to reason about if GUI and services are not separated.
success_criteria: The GUI can build a scenario from map and waypoint inputs and persist it.

phases.name: DroneFactory and first simulation adapter
objectives: Define SimulationBackend.run(scenario, run_config) -> RunResult.
Wrap drone_mc.run_simulation as InHouseMpcQuadBackend.
Normalize outputs to a common schema.
Preserve Monte Carlo style background execution through a generic run manager.
implementation_notes: Adapterize the existing quad dynamics before rewriting fidelity.
Export units metadata with each RunResult.
risks: The initial quad sim is altitude-simplified and accepts 2D waypoints, so 3D path behavior needs staged upgrades.
success_criteria: A ScenarioSpec can run through the in-house backend and return a normalized RunResult.

phases.name: Replay, analysis, and run storage
objectives: Save each run as metadata JSON plus trajectory CSV.
Implement replay controls for play/pause, scrubbing, speed, reference vs actual, markers, and terrain clearance.
Add analysis views for tracking error, kinematics, attitude, control effort, miss distance, Monte Carlo envelopes, and parameter summary.
implementation_notes: Replay must work from saved run files without rerunning simulation.
Use a run index to browse historical runs and compare them.
risks: Time-series formats can drift if field units are not explicit.
success_criteria: A completed run can be saved, listed, reloaded, and displayed in replay/analysis views.

phases.name: Higher-fidelity quadcopter model
objectives: Add rotor allocation, motor lag, thrust/torque coefficients, saturation, drag, wind/gusts, battery effects, sensor noise, terrain collision, and better integration to the in-house quad model.
Evaluate optional gym-pybullet-drones or PyFlyt backend after the factory contract is stable.
implementation_notes: Start with in-house fidelity for transparent control and deterministic outputs.
Add PyBullet/PyFlyt as optional backends, not required dependencies.
risks: High-fidelity physics may require different controllers and smaller time steps.
success_criteria: Backends remain swappable through the same RunResult contract.

phases.name: Fixed-wing / Cessna backend
objectives: Add JSBSimCessnaBackend using JSBSim Python bindings.
Bridge waypoints to heading, altitude, speed, or direct surface commands.
Normalize JSBSim properties to RunResult.
implementation_notes: Share scenario and result schemas but keep backend-specific controller settings.
Use JSBSim aircraft XML and property tree instead of hand-authoring Cessna aerodynamic coefficients.
risks: JSBSim installation and model paths may be platform-sensitive on Windows.
success_criteria: A fixed-wing scenario runs through JSBSim and appears in replay and analysis views.

phases.name: UX polish and packaging
objectives: Add project browser, recent scenarios, validation warnings, run queue, console, parameter presets, import/export, screenshots, and report export.
Package the app with a reproducible environment and simple launch command.
Add smoke tests for scenario loading, map conversion, simulation, run export/import, and replay.
implementation_notes: Keep long-running simulation work in background workers and stream progress into the UI.
Keep UI layout organized around Create Map, Replay 3D, Analysis, Runs, and Console tabs.
risks: Visualization complexity can overwhelm the main workflow if controls are not staged.
success_criteria: A user can create a scenario, run it, save it, replay it, and inspect analysis without editing code.

in_house_mpc_quad.use_for: First integrated backend and transparent controller experimentation.
limitations: Current path input is effectively 2D plus target altitude
No terrain collision or wind model yet

pybullet_or_pyflyt_quad.use_for: Optional higher-fidelity collision-aware quadcopter simulation after contracts stabilize.
strengths: Useful for reinforcement learning and multi-agent work
limitations: Controller bridging may require new adapters
Visualization and physics coordinates need careful alignment

jsbsim_cessna.use_for: Small fixed-wing aircraft simulation using a standard open-source 6DOF FDM.
strengths: Aircraft aerodynamics, propulsion, control surfaces, and atmosphere are built in
Python bindings expose a property tree
Cessna examples and scripts are available
limitations: Aircraft model setup differs from quadcopter dynamics
Windows package/model-path setup should be validated early

layout: Left sidebar for project/scenario selection, map controls, vehicle model, waypoints, markers, sim parameters, and run buttons.
Main tabs for Create Map, Replay 3D, Analysis, Runs, and Console.
Mode toggles for waypoint editing versus marker editing.
Progress indicators and cancel buttons for long-running simulation tasks.
interaction_model: Scenario editing updates a central state object.
Run buttons serialize current scenario state before dispatching a backend job.
Replay and analysis tabs consume saved RunResult artifacts.

storage_recommendations.scenarios: Store versioned scenario JSON files under scenarios/.
storage_recommendations.maps: Cache map metadata, processed terrain arrays, and source tile imagery under maps/.
storage_recommendations.runs: Store one directory per run under runs/ with metadata JSON and trajectory CSV.
storage_recommendations.exports: Use exports/ for reports, screenshots, static HTML, and external data handoff.

key_risks: Coordinate-frame mistakes between WGS84, local ENU, terrain elevation, and simulator axes.
GUI callbacks becoming tightly coupled to simulator-specific fields.
JSBSim and PyBullet/PyFlyt dependency installation complexity on Windows.
Map tile provider rate limits, API keys, and cache invalidation.
High-fidelity models may need controllers that differ significantly from the current MPC controller.
immediate_next_actions: Create a scenario manager for saving/loading JSON scenarios.
Wrap the current drone_mc backend behind a common simulation interface.
Add a first Panel GUI workflow for creating, running, replaying, and analyzing scenarios.
